task.name: Backend task reporter
task.objective: Compose a complete, evidence-backed task report and submit it to the tmux-cli backend via the task-report MCP tool. The MCP tool hard-rejects missing fields, contentless stubs, and out-of-enum values with NO coercion — this command's job is to derive every field properly BEFORE the wire call so a rejection never happens.

glossary.term: The problem being reported. Resolved from: (1) $ARGUMENTS free-form text, (2) a path in $ARGUMENTS to a saved outbox draft (.tmux-cli/task-reports/outbox-*.md) for retry, (3) the current conversation context when $ARGUMENTS is empty.
Optional. A goal-\d+ token found in PROBLEM or the conversation, used to locate on-disk evidence. Absent for non-goal reports.
.tmux-cli/task-reports/ — where a composed report is saved when the backend is unreachable, so the work is never lost.

llm.mandate: NEVER submit a stub proposed_fix ("fix it", "tbd", "n/a", "none", "not applicable", "to be determined") — the backend rejects them; derive a concrete remediation or name the smallest concrete diagnostic step
NEVER invent evidence — quote verbatim from files/logs or omit the Evidence section line; paraphrased "evidence" is worse than none
NEVER use a category or severity outside the closed sets in step 3 — the backend rejects them with NO coercion
NEVER modify any project file — the only writes allowed are OUTBOX_DIR drafts on submission failure
One report per invocation — never batch multiple distinct problems into one report; tell the user to re-invoke for the second problem

step.title: Preflight: reporting enabled
step.action: Read .tmux-cli/setting.yaml and resolve API enablement: flat `apiEnabled` key wins when present, else nested `api.enabled`.

check.action: Print: "Task reporting is disabled — set api.enabled: true in .tmux-cli/setting.yaml (or run tmux-cli setting) and retry."

step.title: Resolve the problem statement
step.action: If $ARGUMENTS is a path matching .tmux-cli/task-reports/outbox-*.md, read that draft: it already contains the six fields. If it carries an `## Invalid Fields:` line (saved after a backend invalid-input rejection), re-derive exactly the named fields per step 3, drop the marker line, then go to step 5. Otherwise (saved on network/backend unreachability) skip to step 5 and submit it verbatim.
Else set PROBLEM from $ARGUMENTS, or — when empty — from the most recent concrete problem discussed in this conversation.

check.action: Print: "Nothing to report — pass a problem statement: /tmux:task-report <what went wrong>"

step.title: Gather evidence from on-disk artifacts
step.action: Scan PROBLEM and conversation for a goal-\d+ token; if found, set GOAL_ID.

check.action: Read {GOAL_DIR}/signal.json — the verdict, owner, and per-finding detail (failing_command, output_excerpt, expected_state).
Read the highest-numbered {GOAL_DIR}/corrections/cycle-N.md — the last correction the implementer saw.
Read {GOAL_DIR}/runbook.md if present — the ops remedy for a parked goal.
Read the GOAL_ID entry in .tmux-cli/goals.yaml — status, retry counters, failed_by/blocked_by.
Read the tail (last ~50 lines) of .tmux-cli/logs/taskvisor.log filtered to GOAL_ID lines.
Read the tails of .tmux-cli/logs/panes/*{ns}*.log for the goal's namespace if pane logs exist.

step.action: For non-goal problems: collect verbatim error output, failing command, and file:line references already present in the conversation.
step.rule: Evidence is QUOTED, never paraphrased. Cap each excerpt at ~15 lines; prefer the lines that show the failure over surrounding noise.

row.when: planner/spec defect: contradictory or under-specified goal, bad plan, wrong acceptance/validate rules
daemon/dispatch/scheduling: taskvisor tick, dispatch, window lifecycle, budgets, cascade, worktrees
validation infrastructure: validator/investigator behavior, validate.sh handling, verdict classification
implementer/code: worker behavior, code defects in produced work, execute.xml protocol
anything else: install, setup, docs, ideas, backend itself
blocks goals outright, cascades the DAG, loses work or data, no workaround
degraded behavior or wasted budget with a known workaround

substep.title: title
substep.rule: One line, ≤80 chars, names the specific mechanism — "GateTerminalPass downgrades pass on stale scriptPassed", never "validation is broken".

substep.title: description — fixed skeleton
proposed_fix
substep.rule: Concrete remediation at file/function/config level when the cause is known (e.g. "re-run validate.sh in rerunValidationOnly before GateTerminalPass").

When the cause is unknown, name the smallest concrete diagnostic step (e.g. "add reason logging to runValidateScript non-pass paths, then re-trigger") — that is actionable; "investigate" alone is not.

check.condition: derived proposed_fix lowercases/trims to one of: '', 'fix it', 'none', 'n/a', 'na', 'not applicable', 'to be determined', 'tbd'
check.action: Rewrite it — derive from the Evidence section what change or diagnostic would settle the issue. Do NOT submit until this gate passes.

substep.title: expected_green_state
substep.rule: An observable, checkable condition — a command that passes, a log line that appears, a status that changes. Prefer a runnable command in backticks.

step.title: Assemble optional payload
step.action: Build a payload map with machine context: goal_id, cycle, window names, key log excerpts — only fields that exist.
step.rule: Payload is bounded: ≤10KB total; truncate excerpts rather than dropping the submission.

step.title: Submit
step.action: Call the task-report MCP tool with: category, severity, title, description, proposed_fix, expected_green_state, payload.

check.action: The error names the failing field(s). Fix exactly those fields per step 3 and resubmit ONCE. If it fails again, fall through to the outbox branch — and append a final line `## Invalid Fields: {comma-separated field names from the last error}` to the draft, so the retry re-derives them instead of resubmitting a payload the backend already rejected (a verbatim retry of an invalid draft can never succeed).
Print the step-0 enable instruction and STOP.
Write the full composed report (all six fields + payload as a fenced yaml block) to {OUTBOX_DIR}/outbox-{UTC timestamp yyyymmdd-hhmmss}.md (mkdir -p the directory).
Print: "Backend unreachable — report saved to {path}. Retry with: /tmux:task-report {path}"
Print the report summary (category/severity/title) followed by: "Reported: task id={id} status={status}. Track it with /tmux:task-list."

execution-rules.rule: Local validation mirrors the MCP tool exactly: six non-empty fields, no stub proposed_fix, closed category/severity sets — a wire rejection means this spec was not followed
Evidence verbatim, bounded excerpts, sources named
Only writes allowed: OUTBOX_DIR drafts on submission failure
One problem per report; one resubmit attempt on invalid-input, then outbox WITH the `## Invalid Fields:` marker — never park a known-invalid draft for verbatim retry
